items.title: Program structure and main: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? C++ permits void main as a portable alternative whenever the operating system ignores exit status.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. A portable hosted C++ entry point returns int; use int main() or a supported argument signature and communicate failure with a nonzero status.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. A portable hosted C++ entry point returns int; use int main() or a supported argument signature and communicate failure with a nonzero status.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: A portable hosted C++ entry point returns int; use int main() or a supported argument signature and communicate failure with a nonzero status.
C++ permits void main as a portable alternative whenever the operating system ignores exit status.

answer.explanation: A portable hosted C++ entry point returns int; use int main() or a supported argument signature and communicate failure with a nonzero status.

items.title: Program structure and main: complete the language rule

items.title: Program structure and main: executable application
items.prompt: Read one integer in a hosted C++ program, print value plus one followed by a newline, and finish with successful main semantics.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
